title: GTCX Sealed, Hardened, Institutionalized, and Provisioned Protocol
purpose: Provide the final release-management gate after DSLC has passed: every v1 release must be sealed with verified proof, hardened against critical security/legal/ops gaps, institutionalized to GTCX v1 product standards, and provisioned with versioned specs, surfaces, docs, and changelog.

dslcRelationship.rule: DSLC establishes release-to-revenue readiness. SHIP consumes the DSLC decision as its prerequisite and never downgrades DSLC blockers.

qascRelationship.rule: QASC proof is consumed through DSLC and may also be referenced directly in the sealed proof pack. SHIP does not rescore QASC.

sealed.definition: Verified proof of all required protocols, release decisions, evidence chains, and release-manager attestation.

controls.description: QASC, DSLC, MPR/SIGNAL, test, evidence, and release reports are linked in one proof pack.
DSLC decision witness is ready at 100/100 for the same release unit.
Evidence files are current, machine-readable, and trace to the release commit or version.
Release manager records the final ship attestation and rollback boundary.

hardened.definition: Critical security, legal, and operational vulnerabilities meet control requirements before public/customer activation.

controls.description: Critical and high security controls have passing evidence or approved exceptions.
Legal, privacy, data-sharing, IP, terms, and agreement blockers are closed by the authorized human owner.
Deploy, rollback, observability, incident, support, and operational readiness controls meet the release class.
Any residual security/legal/ops exceptions have named owner, expiry, rationale, and evidence.

institutionalized.definition: The release adheres to institutional-grade GTCX v1 product standards across experience, billing, onboarding, docs, and architecture.

controls.description: Producer/operator experience is documented, tested, and supportable.
Payment, billing, customer management, revenue ops, and FinOps paths are defined or explicitly out of scope.
Customer/operator onboarding, success, support, and handoff docs exist.
Architecture, runbook, API/CLI, user docs, and institutional documentation standards are satisfied.

provisioned.definition: Specs, features, surfaces, docs, GitBook/public releases, and changelog are version controlled and formally issued.
provisioned.owner: release manager, docs owner, repo owner

controls.description: Formal specs are versioned, machine-readable where applicable, and referenced from the release manifest.
SDK, CLI, desktop, cloud, mobile, and other shipped surfaces/features are versioned or explicitly not applicable.
GitBook and public-facing docs are formally issued for the release class.
Public changelog, release notes, and version history are formally issued or approved as non-public.

decisionPolicy.readyRule: DSLC is ready at 100/100 and every applicable SHIP control in every required pillar is satisfied or has an approved not-applicable determination.
decisionPolicy.notApplicableRule: A required control may be marked not-applicable only with rationale, named approver, and evidence of the applicability determination.
decisionPolicy.postDslcBoundary: SHIP is the final release-management check after DSLC. It can block formal shipment even when DSLC passed, but it cannot override a DSLC blocker.
decisionPolicy.claimBoundary: SHIP records legal, public-release, billing, and product-readiness approvals; an agent cannot synthesize a legal signature, revenue-recognition approval, public claim, or human release-manager attestation.
decisionPolicy.segregationOfDuties: The same actor must not both originate and sovereignly approve a legal instrument, public disclosure, pricing exception, revenue-recognition decision, or final release-manager attestation.

rollout.rule: A repository may claim v1 shipped only after QASC, DSLC, and SHIP evidence exist for the same release unit.
